content.title: Presentarse uno mismo y a otros
content.data:
Me
Hello, my name is Andres. I am 25 years old and I am from Colombia. I live in Bucaramanga.
I am a student and I like learning languages, listening to music and playing football. Nice to
meet you.

My friend Laura
This is my friend Laura. She is from Mexico and she is 23 years old. She is a teacher and
she lives in Monterrey. She likes dancing and reading books. We are good friends.

My father
This is my father. His name is Carlos. He is 52 years old. He is tall and he has short black
hair. He is an engineer and he works in a big company. He likes reading newspapers and
watching football.

My mother
This is my mother. Her name is Maria. She is 48 years old. She is short and she has long
brown hair. She is a teacher and she works at a school. She likes cooking and listening to
music.

My older brother
This is my older brother. His name is Juan. He is 28 years old. He is tall and strong. He is a
doctor and he works in a hospital. He likes playing basketball and travelling.

My younger sister
This is my younger sister. Her name is Sofia. She is 17 years old. She is beautiful and she
has long black hair. She is a student at high school. She likes dancing and using her phone.

My grandmother
This is my grandmother. Her name is Rosa. She is 75 years old. She is small and very kind.
She doesn’t work now. She likes watching TV, watering her plants and telling stories.

My grandfather
This is my grandfather. His name is Luis. He is 78 years old. He is short and he has white
hair. He doesn’t work now. He likes gardening and playing chess.

My uncle
This is my uncle. His name is Pedro. He is 45 years old. He is tall and thin. He is a mechanic
and he works in a garage. He likes fixing cars and watching movies.

My aunt
This is my aunt. Her name is Ana. She is 42 years old. She is short and friendly. She is a
nurse and she works in a hospital. She likes baking cakes and shopping.

My cousin (male)
This is my cousin. His name is Diego. He is 19 years old. He is young and tall. He is a
university student. He likes playing video games and listening to music.

My cousin (female)
This is my cousin. Her name is Camila. She is 16 years old. She is pretty and has long hair.
She is a high school student. She likes dancing and taking photos.

My son
This is my son. His name is Mateo. He is 8 years old. He is small and very active. He is a
student. He likes playing football and drawing pictures.

My daughter
This is my daughter. Her name is Emma. She is 5 years old. She is beautiful and has curly
hair. She goes to kindergarten. She likes playing with dolls and singing.

My nephew
This is my nephew. His name is Lucas. He is 10 years old. He is energetic and smart. He is
a student. He likes riding his bike and playing sports.

My niece
This is my niece. Her name is Isabella. She is 7 years old. She is cute and cheerful. She is a
student. She likes reading stories and painting.

My brother-in-law
This is my brother-in-law. His name is David. He is 30 years old. He is tall and strong. He is
a police officer. He likes running and watching football.

My sister-in-law
This is my sister-in-law. Her name is Laura. She is 29 years old. She is friendly and has
brown hair. She is a graphic designer. She likes yoga and travelling.

My father-in-law
This is my father-in-law. His name is Roberto. He is 55 years old. He is serious and
hardworking. He is a businessman. He likes drinking coffee and reading.

My mother-in-law
This is my mother-in-law. Her name is Carmen. She is 53 years old. She is kind and
talkative. She is a housewife. She likes cooking and spending time with family.

My son-in-law
This is my son-in-law. His name is Andrés. He is 26 years old. He is responsible and friendly.
He is an architect. He likes playing guitar.

My daughter-in-law
This is my daughter-in-law. Her name is Sofia. She is 24 years old. She is beautiful and
patient. She is a teacher. She likes reading and taking care of the family.

content.title: Preguntando y respondiendo preguntas personales
content.data:
What’s your name?
My name is Andres Mauricio.

How old are you?
I am 28 years old.

Where are you from?
I am from Colombia. I live in Bucaramanga.

What’s your job?
I am a student. / I work as a graphic designer.

What’s your phone number?
My phone number is 300-123-4567.

What’s your email?
My email is andres.mauricio@gmail.com.

Where do you live?
I live in Bucaramanga, Colombia.

Do you have brothers or sisters?
Yes, I have one brother and one sister.

Are you married?
No, I am single. / Yes, I am married.

What do you do in your free time?
I like listening to music, playing football and learning languages.

Do you have any pets?
Yes, I have a dog. His name is Max.

What’s your favourite colour?
My favourite colour is blue.

What’s your nationality?

I am Colombian.
Do you speak other languages?
Yes, I speak Spanish and a little English.

What do you like doing at the weekend?
I like going out with my friends and watching movies.

content.title: Sección extra:
content.data:
Hello, my name is Andres. I am Colombian. Excuse me, how are you? I’m fine, thank you.
And you? Nice to meet you. Thank you very much.

This is my friend James. He is American and he is married. “How are you, James?” “I’m fine,
thank you. And you?” “I’m very well, thanks.”

Good morning. Excuse me, my name is Laura and I am Spanish. I am single. Nice to meet
you. Have a nice day!

This is my teacher, Mrs. Sophie. She is French and she is married. “How are you today?”
“I’m fine, thank you. And you?” “No problem, I’m good.”

Hello, I am Hiroshi. I am Japanese and I am single. Sorry, can you repeat your name,
please? Thank you very much. See you later.

This is my neighbour, Mr. Rossi. He is Italian and he is divorced. “Good afternoon, how are
you?” “I’m fine, thanks. And you?” “You’re welcome.”

Hi, my name is Camila. I am Brazilian. I am married and I have two children. Excuse me,
what’s your name? Nice to meet you. Have a nice day!

Good night, Mom. I am going to bed now. “How are you?” “I’m fine, thank you.” “Sleep well.
See you tomorrow.”

This is my British friend, Emma. She is single and she lives in London. “Excuse me, how are
you today?” “I’m very well, thanks. And you?”

Sorry, I’m late. Hello, my name is Li Wei and I am Chinese. I am married. Thank you for
waiting for me. You’re welcome.

“Good evening. How are you?” “I’m fine, thank you. And you?” “I’m okay, thanks.” My name
is Carlos and I am Colombian.

This is my aunt Patricia. She is widowed and she is American. She is very kind. “Have a nice
day, auntie.” “Thank you, dear. See you later.”

Excuse me, please. I am Pierre and I am French. I am single. Nice to meet you. Thank you
for your help.

This is my Italian friend Marco. He is married and he has a big family. “How are you, Marco?”
“I’m fine, thanks. And you?” “No problem, I’m great.”

Good morning, everyone. My name is Yuna and I am Japanese. I am single. Have a nice
day and see you tomorrow in class.

“Sorry, can you help me, please?” “Of course. How are you?” “I’m fine, thank you. And you?”
My name is Ana and I am Spanish.

This is my Brazilian colleague, Rafael. He is divorced. “Good afternoon, how are you?” “I’m
very well, thanks. You’re welcome.”

Hello, I am David. I am British and I am married. Excuse me, what time is it, please? Thank
you very much. Have a nice evening.

This is my Chinese friend, Mei. She is widowed and she lives with her son. “How are you
today?” “I’m fine, thank you. See you later.”

Good night, everyone. My name is Sofia and I am Italian. I am single. Thank you for a lovely
evening. See you tomorrow and have a nice day!

content.title: La hora
content.data:
What time is it?
It’s three o’clock.

What time is it now?
It’s half past four.

Excuse me, what time is it?
It’s quarter past seven.

Do you know what time it is?
Yes, it’s twenty past nine.

What time is it, please?
It’s quarter to ten.

What’s the time?
It’s five to eight.

Can you tell me the time?
Sure, it’s ten past eleven.

What time is it right now?

It’s half past two.

Excuse me, what’s the time?
It’s twenty-five to six.

What time is it?
It’s twelve o’clock.

Variaciones útiles:

It’s five past three.
It’s twenty-five past one.
It’s ten to four.

content.title: Días de la semana
content.data:
Today is Monday.

I have English class on Tuesday.

Wednesday is my busiest day.

On Thursday I play football.

Friday is my favourite day.

We go to the cinema on Saturday.

On Sunday I visit my family.

content.title: Meses del año
content.data:
My birthday is in January.

February is the shortest month.

In March the weather starts to get warmer.

April is a very rainy month.

I travel every year in May.

June is my favourite month.

It is very hot in July.

In August many people go on holiday.

September is the beginning of the school year.

October is a beautiful month with many colours.

It is cold in November.

December is the month of Christmas.

content.title: Estaciones del año
content.data:
There are four seasons in a year.

Spring is my favourite season.

In summer I go to the beach.

It is very hot in summer.

Autumn is cool and colourful.

The leaves fall in autumn.

Winter is the coldest season.

In winter I wear warm clothes.

I like spring because of the flowers.

Summer is good for swimming and travelling.

content.title: Cumpleaños
content.data:
My birthday is on the 15th of March.

Today is the 30th of January.

When is your birthday?

Her birthday is on July 22nd.

I was born on the 5th of November 1997.

What date is it today?

My mother’s birthday is in December.

We celebrate my son’s birthday on the 10th of April.

His birthday is next week.

My sister was born on the first of August.

Expresiones útiles extra:

Happy Birthday!

When is your mother’s birthday?

I always have a party on my birthday.

content.title: Sección extra:
content.data:
Today is the fifteenth of March. It is my birthday. I always wake up early in the morning and
have breakfast at eight o’clock. Yesterday was my last day at work last month.

Tomorrow is the twenty-first of June. It is the first day of summer. I usually get up at seven in
the morning and go to bed at midnight.

Last week I had a doctor’s appointment on the tenth of April. It was in the afternoon at three
o’clock. The day after tomorrow I have another appointment in the morning.

My sister’s birthday is on the thirty-first of December. She always celebrates it at night. Last
month she turned twenty-five years old.

Every day I wake up at half past six in the morning. I have breakfast at seven o’clock and I
usually finish work at midday.

Next week is very important for me. On the fifth of May I have an English exam in the
afternoon. I need to study hard every evening.

Yesterday was the third of February. It was a very cold day. I stayed at home all day and
went to bed early at night.

My mother’s birthday is on the twenty-first of July. We always have a family dinner in the
evening. Last year we celebrated it at a nice restaurant.

Today is the first of January. It is New Year’s Day. Many people celebrate at midnight with
their family and friends.

The day after tomorrow is the fifteenth of August. I am going to the beach with my friends in
the morning. We plan to return home in the evening.

Last month I visited my grandparents on the tenth of March. I arrived at their house at noon
and stayed until late in the afternoon.

I usually go to English class three times a week in the evening. Next week my teacher is
going to give us a test on the fourth of April.
My son was born on the fifth of November at midnight. Today he is celebrating his fourth
birthday. We are having a small party in the afternoon.

Yesterday I woke up very late at ten o’clock in the morning. It was the second day of my
holidays last week.

On the thirty-first of October we celebrate Halloween. Children usually go out at night to ask
for candy.

Next week my brother is arriving from Spain on the twenty-first of May. His flight arrives at
midday, so I will pick him up at the airport in the afternoon.

Today is the tenth of September. It is the first day of school for my daughter. She starts
classes at seven thirty in the morning.

Last week I had a job interview on the fifteenth of March in the morning. I arrived early at
nine o’clock and everything went well.

My wedding anniversary is on the third of August. Every year we go out for dinner in the
evening to celebrate.

Tomorrow is the fourth of July. It is an important holiday in the United States. Many American
people celebrate it with fireworks at night.

content.title: Personales
content.data:
I am from Colombia.
You are my best friend.
He is very tall.
She is beautiful.
It is my house.
We are happy.
They are my family.

content.title: Posesivos
content.data:
My name is Andres.
Your book is on the table.
His car is black.
Her hair is long.
Its color is blue.
Our house is big.
Their children are friendly.

Demostrativo
This is my phone.
That is my teacher.
These are my shoes.
Those are my parents.

content.title: Objeto Pronombre
content.data:
Can you help me?
I like you.
I know him.
I love her.
I don’t like it.
She is teaching us English.
I see them every day.

content.title: Interrogativo
content.data:
Who is she?
What is your name?
Where do you live?
When is your birthday?
How are you?
Whose bag is this?

content.title: Indefinido
content.data:
I want to eat something.
She needs something to drink.
I know nothing about football.
He has nothing in his pockets.
She knows everything.
They like everything here.
Everyone is happy today.
Everyone loves music.
Somebody is at the door.
Someone called me last night.
Nobody is in the classroom.
No one understands the question.

content.title: Sección extra:
content.data:
Excuse me, whose bag is this? Somebody left it on the table. I don’t know whose it is, but it
looks expensive.

This is my sister’s house. “Whose car is parked outside?” “It’s my father’s car. Someone
came to visit us last night.”

“Whose phone is ringing?” “It’s mine, sorry.” Nobody answers the phone when I’m busy.
In my classroom, somebody always forgets their books. Today the teacher asked, “Whose
notebook is this on the floor?”

“Whose jacket is on the chair?” “I think it’s John’s. Someone left it here after the meeting
yesterday.”

My mother is looking for her keys. “Has anybody seen my keys?” she asks. Nobody knows
where they are.

“Whose birthday is on the fifteenth of March?” “It’s my cousin’s birthday. Someone is having
a party for her.”

I found a wallet in the street. “Whose wallet is this?” I asked. Nobody answered, so I took it
to the police station.

“Whose children are playing in the garden?” “They are my neighbours’ children. Someone is
having a birthday party today.”

At work, somebody always drinks my coffee. “Whose cup is this?” I asked. No one answered
me.

“Is this anybody’s pencil?” “No, it’s not mine.” Nobody knows who left it on my desk.
This is a photo of my family. “Whose baby is this?” my friend asked. “It’s my sister’s baby,” I
answered.

“Has anyone seen my glasses?” “Sorry, nobody has seen them.” I always lose things at
home.

“Whose turn is it to clean the kitchen?” “I don’t know. Somebody forgot to make the schedule
this week.”

My grandmother asked, “Whose shoes are these by the door?” “They’re mine, Grandma.
Sorry!”

In the office, someone is always using my computer. “Whose email is open?” I asked.
Nobody said anything.

“Whose homework is missing?” the teacher asked. “Nobody did the homework last week,”
answered one student.

“Is this anybody’s water bottle?” “Yes, it’s mine. Thank you!” Somebody always leaves their
things in the classroom.

We are looking at old family photos. “Whose wedding is this?” “It’s my parents’ wedding.
They got married thirty years ago.”

“Can anybody help me? I can’t find my keys.” “Sorry, nobody saw them. Maybe someone
took them by mistake.”

content.title: Describiendo personas
content.data:
This is my father. He is 52 years old. He is tall and thin. He has short black hair and brown
eyes. He is a very kind and intelligent man.

This is my mother. She is 48 years old. She is short and a little heavy. She has long brown
hair and beautiful green eyes. She is friendly and very patient.

This is my older brother. He is 28 years old. He is tall and strong. He has short black hair
and dark eyes. He is funny and very hardworking.

This is my younger sister. She is 17 years old. She is medium height and slim. She has long
straight hair and big brown eyes. She is cheerful and a bit shy.

This is my best friend. His name is David. He is 25 years old. He is tall and athletic. He has
curly brown hair and blue eyes. He is outgoing and very funny.

This is my grandmother. She is 75 years old. She is short and a little fat. She has white hair
and kind eyes. She is very sweet and generous.

This is my teacher. She is 35 years old. She is medium height and slim. She has blonde hair
and green eyes. She is intelligent and very patient with students.

This is my cousin. He is 19 years old. He is tall and thin. He has short black hair and wears
glasses. He is quiet but very friendly.

This is my neighbour. She is 40 years old. She is short and has a nice smile. She has curly
red hair and brown eyes. She is energetic and very kind.

This is my uncle. He is 45 years old. He is medium height and strong. He has short grey hair
and dark eyes. He is serious but very helpful.

content.title: Colores y ropa
content.data:
My favorite t-shirt is blue and white.

She is wearing a red dress and black shoes.

He has a green jacket and brown trousers.

I like my yellow sweater and grey socks.

The girl is wearing a pink skirt and white sneakers.

My brother wears an orange hoodie and blue jeans.

Today I have a purple blouse and black pants.

He is wearing a brown coat and dark blue scarf.

Her new hat is red and her gloves are grey.

I bought a white shirt and navy trousers.

She likes her light green dress and beige sandals.

My father wears a black suit and white shirt.

The baby has a yellow t-shirt and blue shorts.

I’m wearing my favorite pink hoodie and black leggings.

He has new brown boots and green socks.

My sister is wearing an orange skirt and white top.

This purple jacket is very warm and beautiful.

She always wears red lipstick with her black dress.

I need a grey sweater and blue jeans for tomorrow.

The boy is wearing a yellow cap and red t-shirt.

My father, my mother and my older brother live together in a big house, while my younger
sister and I visit them every weekend.

My grandfather and my grandmother are 78 and 75 years old, and they love spending time
with their grandchildren, especially my son Mateo and my daughter Emma.

My uncle Pedro, my aunt Ana, and their son Diego (my cousin) always come to our family
parties with my nephew Lucas and my niece Isabella.

My brother-in-law David and my sister-in-law Laura are very friendly, and they often visit my
father-in-law and mother-in-law on Sundays.

My son Mateo and my daughter Emma like playing with their cousin Camila, while my
son-in-law Andrés and my daughter-in-law Sofia help my grandmother in the garden every
month.

Describiendo la casa y la ciudad

There is a big living room in my house.
There are two bedrooms in my apartment.
There is a kitchen next to the dining room.
There is a comfortable bed in my room.
There are many books on my desk.
There is a big window in my bedroom.
There are two bathrooms in my house.
There is a beautiful garden behind my house.
There are many cars in my city.
There is a big park near my house.
There are tall buildings in the centre of the city.
There is a supermarket in front of my building.
There are many shops in my neighbourhood.
There is a small balcony in my apartment.
There are pictures on the walls of my room.
There is a television in the living room.
There are three chairs and one table in the kitchen.
There is a river in the south of my city.
There are many restaurants near my house.
There is a quiet street behind my building.
There is a big patio behind my house.
There is a garage next to the house.
There is a beautiful garden in front of my house.
There are two bedrooms in my apartment.
There is a modern kitchen and a dining room.
There is a comfortable living room with a big sofa.
There is a small bathroom on the first floor.
There is a balcony in my bedroom.
There are many windows in my room.
There is a quiet terrace on the roof.
There is a big park near my house.
There are many shops in my neighbourhood.
There is a supermarket in front of my building.
There are tall buildings in the centre of the city.
There is a river in the south of my city.
There are many restaurants and cafés near my house.
There is a cinema and a shopping mall in my city.
There are wide streets and avenues in the downtown area.
There is a school and a hospital close to my neighbourhood.
There is a football field in the park near my house.

Sección extra

My father is tall and thin. He has short black hair, brown eyes and a big nose. He is a serious
and hardworking man. He usually wears a dark blue suit, a white shirt and a red tie.

My mother is short and slim. She has long dark hair, beautiful green eyes and small hands.
She is very friendly and talkative. Today she is wearing a light blue blouse and a beige skirt.

My older brother is tall and overweight. He has curly brown hair and big ears. He is funny
and honest. In summer he likes wearing shorts, trainers and a light green t-shirt.

My younger sister is short and slim. She has long straight black hair and dark eyes. She is
shy and quiet. She loves wearing her violet skirt and silver earrings.

My best friend Lucas is tall and thin. He has short blond hair and blue eyes. He is very nice
and hardworking. He always wears dark blue jeans, a black jacket and black boots.

My grandmother is short and a little fat. She has white hair, kind eyes and small hands. She
is nice and friendly. At night she wears comfortable pyjamas and warm socks.

My English teacher is tall and slim. She has long red hair and green eyes. She is serious but
very honest. She often wears a black coat, high heels and a beige scarf.

My neighbour Mr. Garcia is short and overweight. He has grey hair and a big mouth. He is
talkative and funny. He likes wearing a brown jacket and black trousers.

My cousin Sofia is tall and slim. She has long light brown hair and beautiful dark eyes. She is
friendly and hardworking. She loves wearing light green leggings and white trainers.

My uncle Pedro is tall and thin. He has short black hair and big hands. He is quiet and
honest. He usually wears a dark suit, a white shirt and a gold tie.
My little brother is short and thin. He has messy brown hair and big brown eyes. He is very
funny and lazy. He likes wearing blue shorts and his favourite trainers.

My aunt Laura is slim and beautiful. She has long black hair and a small nose. She is
friendly and talkative. She often wears a violet blouse, a black skirt and high heels.

My grandfather is short and a little overweight. He has white hair and kind eyes. He is
serious and hardworking. In winter he wears a warm coat, gloves and a grey scarf.

My friend Emma is tall and slim. She has long curly hair and blue eyes. She is nice and
honest. She likes wearing dark green leggings and silver jewellery.

My boss is tall and overweight. He has short hair and small ears. He is serious and
hardworking. He always wears an expensive black suit and a red tie.

My neighbour’s daughter is short and thin. She has long straight hair and big brown eyes.
She is shy but very friendly. She loves wearing pink flip-flops and light blue shorts.

My sister-in-law is slim and beautiful. She has dark hair and green eyes. She is talkative and
funny. She usually wears a beige coat and black boots in winter.

My Japanese friend Hiroshi is short and thin. He has black hair and dark eyes. He is quiet
and honest. He likes wearing comfortable clothes and white trainers.

My mother-in-law is short and overweight. She has short grey hair and a nice smile. She is
friendly and hardworking. She often wears a dark green blouse and a long skirt.

My classmate Anna is tall and slim. She has long blonde hair, blue eyes and long legs. She
is nice and talkative. She likes wearing a light blue jacket, jeans and black boots.

content.title: Rutinas
content.data:
Every day I wake up at 6:30 in the morning. I get up, go to the bathroom, brush my teeth and
take a shower. Then I get dressed and have breakfast with coffee and eggs. After that, I go
to work by bus. In the afternoon I come back home, have lunch and rest a little. In the
evening I exercise, have dinner with my family and watch TV. Finally, I go to bed at 11
o’clock.

My sister wakes up at 7:00. She washes her face, brushes her hair and puts on her clothes.
She has breakfast quickly and then goes to university. When she returns home, she studies
for two hours, has dinner and talks with us. She usually reads a book before she goes to
sleep at midnight.

I usually get up at 6:00. First, I make my bed, then I brush my teeth and take a shower. I
have a big breakfast with fruit and bread. After breakfast I go to the office. In the evening I
cook dinner, watch the news and relax on the sofa. I always go to bed at 10:30.

My brother wakes up at 5:45. He does exercise, takes a shower and has coffee. He leaves
home at 7:00 to go to work. When he comes back, he plays with his children, has dinner and
helps them with homework. He goes to bed around 11:00.

Every morning I wake up at 7:15. I wash my face, get dressed and have breakfast with my
family. Then I go to my English classes. In the afternoon I return home, have lunch and
study. Later I go for a walk, have dinner and watch videos on my phone. I usually go to bed
at 11:00.

My mother gets up at 6:00 every day. She prepares breakfast for everyone, takes a shower
and gets dressed. She works in the morning and comes back home at 3:00 pm. In the
afternoon she cleans the house, cooks dinner and watches her favourite TV shows. She
goes to bed at 10:00.

I wake up at 6:30, brush my teeth and exercise for twenty minutes. Then I have a healthy
breakfast and go to work by car. After work I go to the gym, come home, have dinner and
talk with my wife. I usually read a little before I go to sleep at 11:30.

My father wakes up very early at 5:30. He drinks coffee, reads the newspaper and gets
ready for work. He returns home at 5:00 pm, rests, has dinner and watches football on TV.
He always goes to bed at 10:00.

Every day I get up at 7:00. I take a long shower, have breakfast and check my phone. Then I
go to university. When I come back I have lunch, do my homework and meet my friends. In
the evening I have dinner and relax. I go to bed around 11:45.

She wakes up at 6:45, makes coffee and prepares breakfast for her children. After sending
them to school, she goes to work. In the afternoon she picks them up, helps with homework,
cooks dinner and spends time with the family. She goes to bed at 10:30.

Every morning I wake up at 6:20 when my alarm rings. I stay in bed for five minutes, then I
get up, make my bed and open the window. I go to the bathroom, brush my teeth, wash my
face and take a hot shower. After that, I get dressed, comb my hair and have a big breakfast
with coffee, toast, eggs and fruit.

My brother wakes up at 5:50. He turns off his alarm, stretches his body and gets out of bed.
He brushes his teeth, shaves his face and takes a quick shower. Then he puts on his work
clothes and has breakfast with black coffee and pancakes. He leaves the house at 7:00.
I usually wake up at 7:00. First, I drink a glass of water, then I do some stretching exercises
for ten minutes. After that, I take a shower, get dressed and put on my shoes. I prepare and
eat a healthy breakfast with oatmeal, banana and orange juice before going to work.

She wakes up at 6:30, kisses her husband and gets up quietly. She washes her face with
cold water, brushes her teeth and does her skincare routine. Then she chooses her clothes,
does her hair and makeup, and has breakfast with tea and avocado toast.

Every day my father wakes up at 5:30. He prays for a few minutes, makes his bed, brushes
his teeth and takes a shower. Afterwards, he drinks coffee while reading the news on his
phone and eats eggs with bread for breakfast. He gets ready and leaves home very early.

I wake up at 6:45. I turn on the light, check my phone and get up slowly. I go to the bathroom
to brush my teeth and have a long shower. Then I iron my clothes, get dressed and sit down
to have a calm breakfast with cereal, milk and fresh fruit.

My sister wakes up at 7:10. She stays under the blanket for a while, then gets up, opens the
curtains and makes her bed. She washes her face, puts on moisturizer, brushes her long
hair and gets dressed. She always has a quick breakfast with yogurt and coffee.

He wakes up at 6:00 sharp. He does morning exercises for fifteen minutes, takes a cold
shower, shaves and gets dressed in his suit. After that, he prepares a strong coffee and eats
a full breakfast with ham, cheese and scrambled eggs.

Every morning I wake up at 6:15. I get up immediately, drink water, brush my teeth and take
a shower with music. Then I choose my outfit for the day, style my hair and have breakfast
while listening to the news. I usually eat toast with butter and drink orange juice.

My mother wakes up at 5:45 to prepare breakfast for the family. She washes her face,
brushes her teeth, gets dressed and makes coffee. She cooks eggs, warms the milk and
sets the table before everyone else wakes up. She only sits to eat breakfast after everyone
is ready.

content.title: Adverbios de frecuencia
content.data:
I always wake up at 6:30 in the morning. I usually make my bed and then I brush my teeth
and take a shower. I sometimes have eggs for breakfast, but I never drink coffee.

My brother usually wakes up at 7:00. He always does some exercise, takes a shower and
gets dressed. He sometimes eats a big breakfast, but he never leaves home without
checking his phone.

She always wakes up early at 6:00. She usually washes her face, brushes her hair and puts
on makeup. She sometimes listens to music while having breakfast, but she never watches
TV in the morning.
Every day I usually get up at 6:45. I always brush my teeth and take a shower. I sometimes
have fruit for breakfast and I never go to work without having something to eat.

My mother always wakes up at 5:45. She usually prepares breakfast for everyone and then
she takes a shower. She sometimes drinks tea, but she never eats heavy food in the
morning.

He usually wakes up at 6:15. He always stretches his body, brushes his teeth and takes a
quick shower. He sometimes reads the news while having breakfast, but he never spends
too much time on his phone.

I always wake up at 7:00. I usually make coffee and have toast with avocado. I sometimes
go for a short walk before work, but I never skip breakfast.

My sister usually gets up at 7:20. She always takes a long shower and does her skincare
routine. She sometimes has cereal for breakfast and she never leaves the house with wet
hair.

My father always wakes up very early. He usually drinks black coffee and reads the
newspaper. He sometimes goes for a run, but he never eats sweets in the morning.

We usually wake up at 6:30. We always brush our teeth and get dressed quickly. We
sometimes have a big family breakfast together, but we never use our phones at the table.

She always wakes up at 6:50. She usually listens to podcasts while she gets ready. She
sometimes has yogurt and fruit, but she never forgets to drink water in the morning.

I usually wake up at 7:10. I always make my bed and organize my room before breakfast. I
sometimes eat eggs and I never start my day without planning what I need to do.

My best friend always wakes up late at 7:30. He usually takes a very fast shower and runs to
catch the bus. He sometimes skips breakfast, but he never misses his morning coffee.

Every morning I usually get up at 6:25. I always wash my face with cold water and then I
have a healthy breakfast. I sometimes check my emails, but I never work before 8 o’clock.

They usually wake up at 6:00. They always exercise together for twenty minutes and then
take a shower. They sometimes have pancakes on weekends, but they never eat fast food
for breakfast.

My cousin always wakes up at 6:40. She usually styles her hair carefully and chooses her
clothes the night before. She sometimes has oatmeal and she never leaves without saying
goodbye to her family.

I usually wake up at 7:05. I always drink a glass of water first and then brush my teeth. I
sometimes listen to the radio while having breakfast and I never rush in the morning.

He always wakes up at 5:55. He usually prepares everything the night before so his morning
is calm. He sometimes reads a book while drinking coffee, but he never watches the news.

My girlfriend usually wakes up at 7:15. She always does her skincare routine and then has
breakfast. She sometimes eats bananas and yogurt, but she never drinks coffee because it
makes her nervous.

We always wake up at 6:30 on weekdays. We usually have a calm morning routine and eat
breakfast together. We sometimes talk about our plans for the day, but we never argue in the
morning.

Gustos y disgustos

I like learning English because it is very useful. I like watching movies in English and
listening to songs. However, I don’t like waking up early in the morning and I don’t like eating
vegetables every day.

My sister likes dancing and listening to pop music. She likes wearing colourful clothes and
taking photos. She doesn’t like cold weather and she doesn’t like studying mathematics.

I like playing football with my friends at the weekend. I also like eating pizza and drinking
cold juice. On the other hand, I don’t like running long distances and I don’t like horror
movies.

My mother likes cooking traditional Colombian food. She likes spending time with the family
and watching romantic TV shows. She doesn’t like loud music and she doesn’t like messy
rooms.

He likes travelling to new places and meeting new people. He likes taking photos and trying
different kinds of food. However, he doesn’t like waiting in long lines and he doesn’t like
waking up very early.

I like reading books in my free time, especially adventure stories. I like sunny days and going
to the beach. I don’t like rainy days because I can’t go outside, and I don’t like eating spicy
food.

My best friend likes playing video games and watching football matches. He likes wearing
sports clothes every day. He doesn’t like doing homework and he doesn’t like eating fish.

She likes animals a lot, especially dogs and cats. She likes walking in the park and taking
care of plants. She doesn’t like crowded places and she doesn’t like fast food.

We like spending time together as a family. We like eating ice cream and watching comedy
movies. We don’t like traffic in the city and we don’t like people who are rude.

My father likes working in the garden and repairing things at home. He likes drinking black
coffee in the morning. He doesn’t like using the phone too much and he doesn’t like modern
music.

content.title: Sección extra:
content.data:
Every day I wake up at six thirty in the morning. I get up, take a shower, get dressed and
comb my hair. In the afternoon I do housework and wash the dishes. At night I cook dinner,
relax and read a book. I usually go to bed at eleven o’clock and fall asleep quickly.

My mother wakes up very early. She gets up, has a shower, puts on makeup and gets
dressed. She always cleans the house in the morning. In the evening she cooks and
sometimes checks social media before she goes to bed.
My brother usually wakes up at seven o’clock. He gets up, takes a shower, shaves and gets
dressed. After university he does homework and then helps with housework. He never goes
to bed before midnight.

My sister wakes up at six forty-five. She gets up, has a shower, puts on makeup and combs
her long hair. In the afternoon she goes shopping and cleans the house. At night she
relaxes, checks social media and falls asleep while reading a book.

I wake up at seven in the morning. I get up, take a shower and get dressed quickly. After
work I do housework and wash the dishes. In the evening I cook, relax and sometimes read
a book before I go to bed.

My father wakes up at five thirty. He gets up, has a shower, shaves and gets dressed. He
never does housework. In the evening he relaxes, watches TV and usually falls asleep on
the sofa.

My best friend is a student. She wakes up at seven fifteen, gets up, takes a shower and puts
on makeup. After classes she does homework and sometimes goes shopping. She always
checks social media before she goes to bed.

Every morning my wife wakes up at six o’clock. She gets up, has a shower, combs her hair
and gets dressed. In the afternoon she cleans the house and cooks. At night she relaxes
and falls asleep early.

My little brother wakes up late. He gets up, takes a shower and gets dressed. He never
shaves yet. After school he does homework, then he plays and checks social media. He
goes to bed at ten o’clock.

I usually wake up at six twenty. I get up, have a shower, comb my hair and get dressed. In
the afternoon I go shopping and wash the dishes. At night I cook, relax and read a book
before I fall asleep.

My grandmother wakes up at six in the morning. She gets up slowly, has a shower and gets
dressed. She sometimes does housework in the afternoon. At night she relaxes, reads a
book and falls asleep early.

My teacher wakes up at six forty. She gets up, takes a shower, puts on makeup and combs
her hair. After work she cleans the house and cooks. She usually relaxes and checks social
media before going to bed.

My cousin is a worker. He wakes up at five fifty, gets up, has a shower and shaves. In the
evening he does housework and washes the dishes. He never falls asleep before midnight.
Every day I wake up at seven. I get up, take a shower, get dressed and comb my hair. After
university I do homework and sometimes go shopping. At night I cook, relax and check
social media.

My sister-in-law wakes up at six thirty. She gets up, has a shower, puts on makeup and gets
dressed. She always cleans the house and cooks in the afternoon. She relaxes and falls
asleep while reading.

My best friend Pablo wakes up at seven ten. He gets up, takes a shower, shaves and gets
dressed. In the evening he does homework and washes the dishes. He usually relaxes and
checks social media at night.

My mother-in-law wakes up early. She gets up, has a shower and combs her hair. She does
a lot of housework and cooks every day. At night she relaxes and goes to bed at ten o’clock.

I wake up at six forty-five. I get up, take a shower, get dressed and comb my hair. After work
I go shopping, clean the house and wash the dishes. I relax, read a book and fall asleep
happily.

My young brother wakes up at seven thirty. He gets up quickly, has a shower and gets
dressed. He does homework in the afternoon and sometimes helps cook. He always checks
social media before he goes to bed.

My husband wakes up at six o’clock. He gets up, takes a shower, shaves and gets dressed.
In the evening he helps clean the house and wash the dishes. He relaxes for a while and
usually falls asleep while watching TV.

content.title: Ordenando comida y bebidas
content.data:
I would like a grilled chicken with rice and a coke, please.
Can I have a cappuccino and a croissant, please?
I want a cheeseburger, fries and a large soda, please.
A latte and a blueberry muffin, please.
Two beers and some chicken wings, please.
I’d like two croissants and a coffee to go, please.
Can I have two scoops of chocolate ice cream, please?
I want fried chicken with rice and a juice, please.
One taco with meat and a soda, please.
I’d like the pasta with chicken and a glass of water, please.
A large pepperoni pizza and two colas, please.
I want a salmon sushi roll and green tea, please.
I’d like a ham and cheese sandwich and an orange juice, please.
A coffee and a sandwich to go, please.
Can I have a chicken sandwich and a bottle of water, please?
I want noodles and a lemonade, please.
Two piña coladas and some fries, please.
A whiskey with coke, please.
One large popcorn and a coke, please.
A protein shake and a tuna sandwich, please.
I’d like a coffee and a cheese sandwich, please.
A soup and an apple juice, please.
A tea and a piece of cake, please.
One hot dog and a soda, please.
I want a burger combo with fries and a drink, please.

“Table for two, please,” I say. “Can I see the menu?” The waiter gives us the menu. I’m
allergic to nuts, so I ask carefully. At the end I say, “Can I have the bill, please?” I leave a
small tip and take the rest away to go.

We sit down and say, “Table for four, please.” The waiter asks what we want to drink. “Can I
see the menu?” my friend says. I’m allergic to seafood, so I choose pasta. We ask for
takeaway for the extra food.

“Excuse me, can I see the menu?” I ask. I order a sandwich and juice. At the end I say, “Can
I have the bill, please?” I pay and leave a tip. The waiter puts the rest in a bag for takeaway.

We go to a restaurant and ask for “Table for two, please.” “Can I see the menu?” my wife
says. She is allergic to milk, so she asks the waiter many questions. We pay the bill and
leave a good tip.

“Table for four, please,” my father says. We sit down and look at the menu. “I’m allergic to
eggs,” my sister tells the waiter. We enjoy the food and ask for the bill at the end.

I go to a fast-food restaurant and say, “Can I see the menu?” I order a burger and fries. “Is
this to go?” the girl asks. “Yes, takeaway, please,” I answer.

“Table for two, please.” “Can I see the menu?” I ask. I’m allergic to gluten, so I choose
carefully. After eating I ask for the bill and leave a tip.

My friends and I sit at the table. “Can I see the menu?” one friend asks. Nobody is allergic to
anything, so we order freely. We pay the bill and say thank you.

“I’d like table for four, please.” The waiter brings the menu. “I’m allergic to fish,” my mother
says. We eat well and ask for the bill with the tip.

I go to a café and say, “Can I see the menu?” I order coffee and cake. At the end I pay the
bill and take the rest home as takeaway.

content.title: De compras
content.data:
How much is this blue t-shirt?
I’d like a large red dress, please.
How much are these black shoes?
Do you have this jacket in medium size?
The white sneakers are fifty dollars.
I need a small black bag, please.
How much does this green sweater cost?
I’m looking for a pair of size 42 blue jeans.
These brown boots are very expensive.
Do you have this shirt in extra large?
How much is the yellow cap?
I want to buy two medium grey hoodies.
Is this purple blouse on sale?
These trousers are too small. Do you have them in large?
How much are the orange socks?
I like the navy blue coat. What size is it?
The pink skirt costs twenty-five dollars.
Do you have this t-shirt in black and in size medium?
How much is one kilo of these red apples?
I’d like to try the dark green jacket in large size, please.

“How much does it cost?” I ask the shop assistant. “Can I try it on?” The trousers are too
small, so I say, “Do you have it in another colour?” Finally I say, “It’s perfect. I’ll take it.”

I see a nice jacket. “What’s the price?” I ask. I try it on but it’s too big. “Do you have it in a
smaller size?” The jacket is expensive, but I like it, so I say, “I’ll take it.”

“How much does this blouse cost?” I ask. “Can I try it on?” It’s too small. “Do you have it in
another colour?” The beige one is nice, so I buy it.

I like the trainers. “What’s the price?” I ask. They are a bit expensive, but I try them on and
they fit well. “I’ll take them,” I say happily.

“Can I try on this coat?” I ask. It’s too big. “Do you have it in a smaller size?” The assistant
helps me. Finally I say, “It’s good. I’ll take it.”

I see beautiful high heels. “How much does it cost?” I ask. They are expensive but very nice.
“I’ll take them,” I decide.

“What’s the price of this scarf?” I ask. I try it on and it’s perfect. “Do you have it in dark
green?” I buy two colours.

The shorts are nice. “Can I try them on?” They are too small. “Do you have them in a larger
size?” I finally find the right ones and say, “I’ll take them.”

“How much does this suit cost?” my father asks. It’s a bit expensive, but he tries it on. “It fits
well. I’ll take it,” he says.

I see a silver belt. “What’s the price?” I ask. It’s not too expensive. I try it on and say, “I’ll take
it, please.”

content.title: Preguntando y dando direcciones
content.data:
Excuse me, where is the nearest supermarket? Go straight ahead for two blocks, turn left at
the traffic lights, and the supermarket is on your right next to the bank.

How do I get to the train station from here? Turn right at the corner and go straight. After
three blocks you will see the station on your left. It’s a big white building.

I’m looking for the post office. Go straight on this street, turn right at the pharmacy, and the
post office is opposite the park.

Where is the nearest hospital, please? Walk two blocks straight ahead, turn left, and you will
see the hospital on your right. It’s a very big building.

Excuse me, how can I get to the museum? Go straight and turn right at the traffic lights. The
museum is next to the big church. You can’t miss it.

I need to find the bathroom in this shopping mall. Go straight and turn left at the escalators.
The bathrooms are on the right, next to the food court.

Where is the closest bank? Turn left here and go straight for one block. The bank is on the
corner, opposite the cinema.

How do I get to the city centre from here? Go straight ahead, turn right at the second street,
and continue walking. The city centre is about ten minutes away.

Excuse me, where is the entrance to the park? Walk straight and turn left after the fountain.
The entrance is right there.

I’m looking for the bus stop. Go straight on this road and turn right at the traffic lights. The
bus stop is in front of the school.

How can I get to the hotel “Central”? Turn left at the corner and go straight for three blocks.
The hotel is on your left, next to a coffee shop.

Where is the nearest pharmacy? It’s very close. Go straight and turn right. The pharmacy is
on the left, beside the supermarket.

Excuse me, I need to find the police station. Go straight ahead, turn left at the big statue,
and the police station will be on your right.

How do I get to the beach from here? Walk straight for five blocks, then turn right and
continue walking. The beach is at the end of the street.
Where is room number 305 in this hotel? Take the elevator to the third floor, turn right, and
room 305 is the third door on your left.

I’m lost. How do I get to the shopping mall? Turn around and go straight. At the big
intersection turn left, and the shopping mall is on your right.

Excuse me, where is the taxi rank? Go straight ahead and turn right at the corner. The taxi
rank is next to the train station.

How can I get to the restaurant “La Bella”? Walk straight on this avenue, turn left after the
bridge, and the restaurant is on your right.

Where is the exit of this building? Go straight down the corridor, turn left at the end, and the
exit is on your right.

Excuse me, I’m looking for the main square. Go straight for two blocks, turn right, and
continue walking. The main square is in front of the cathedral.

“Excuse me, how far is the museum?” “Go straight ahead and turn right at the traffic lights.
It’s near, only five minutes from here.”

“Where is the train station?” I ask. “Go straight ahead, turn left at the corner. The station is
opposite the bank.”

“How do I get to the hospital?” “Turn right at the traffic lights, go straight ahead, and it’s on
the left, next to the park.”

“Excuse me, where is the nearest supermarket?” “It’s not far. Go straight ahead, turn left at
the corner. It’s behind the cinema.”

“How far is the beach?” “It’s a bit far. Go straight ahead, turn right at the traffic lights, then go
between the two big buildings.”

“Where is the post office?” “It’s near. Turn left here, go straight ahead, and it’s on the right,
opposite the school.”

“Can you tell me where the hotel is?” “Go straight ahead and turn right at the corner. The
hotel is behind the shopping mall.”

“How far is the cinema?” “It’s very near. Go straight ahead and it’s on the left, next to the
pharmacy.”

“Where is the bus stop?” “Turn right at the traffic lights and go straight ahead. The bus stop
is in front of the church.”

“Excuse me, how do I get to the park?” “Go straight ahead, turn left at the corner, and the
park is on your right, between the school and the library.”

content.title: Hablando sobre el tiempo
content.data:
Today is a beautiful sunny day. The sky is blue and there are no clouds. It is warm and
perfect to go to the park or walk around the city.

It is raining heavily today. The weather is cold and wet. I don’t like this weather because I
can’t go out and I have to stay at home.

It is very hot this afternoon. The temperature is thirty-five degrees and the sun is very strong.
I feel uncomfortable and I just want to stay inside with the air conditioning.

This morning it is cloudy and cool. It is not raining yet, but I think it is going to rain later. I’m
wearing a light jacket because the weather is fresh.

In winter it is usually very cold in my city. Sometimes it snows and everything becomes
white. I like drinking hot chocolate when the weather is like this.

It is a windy day today. The wind is strong and it moves the trees. I like the fresh air, but it is
difficult to walk with this strong wind.

The weather is perfect today. It is sunny but not too hot. This is my favourite kind of weather
because I can do many outdoor activities.

It is foggy this morning. I can’t see the buildings clearly. The weather is cold and humid, so I
have to drive very carefully.

In summer my city is extremely hot and humid. The temperature can reach forty degrees.
Because of this, many people go to the beach or stay indoors.

It rained all day yesterday. The streets were wet and there were many puddles. Today the
weather is better and the sun is shining again.

Autumn in my country is cool and pleasant. The leaves change colour and the weather is
nice for walking. I enjoy this season very much.

It is snowing right now. Everything is white and beautiful. The temperature is below zero, so I
am wearing my warmest coat and gloves.

The weather forecast says it will be sunny and warm tomorrow. I am happy because I can go
hiking with my friends this weekend.

Today is a stormy day. There is thunder and lightning. I don’t like this weather because it
makes me feel nervous.

In spring the weather is mild and the flowers start to bloom. It rains sometimes but the days
are longer and warmer.

It is very cold outside today. The temperature is only five degrees. I don’t want to leave the
house because I hate this freezing weather.

The weather is changing very fast. It was sunny in the morning, but now it is cloudy and
starting to rain. I always carry an umbrella with me.

Yesterday it was a lovely sunny day with a clear blue sky. I spent the whole afternoon
outside enjoying the good weather.

In my city the weather is very hot and dry during the day, but at night it becomes cool and
pleasant. I like the contrast between day and night.

The weather is terrible today. It is raining heavily and it is very windy. All my plans for today
are cancelled because of this bad weather.

What’s the weather like today? It’s warm and sunny. I love this weather.

It’s cool this morning. I think I need a jacket. What’s the weather like in your city?

It’s freezing today! The temperature is very low and I don’t want to go outside.

It’s humid and hot. I feel tired because of the weather. What’s the weather like tomorrow?

It’s dry and sunny. Perfect weather to go to the beach.

Look at the sky! There is a beautiful rainbow after the thunderstorm.

It’s going to be a big thunderstorm this afternoon. I hope it doesn’t rain too much.

What’s the weather like in London? Is it cool or freezing?

It’s humid and cloudy today. I think it’s going to rain soon.

Yesterday it was cool and pleasant, but today it’s warm and sunny again.

content.title: Haciendo planes
content.data:
Would you like to go to the cinema with me this Saturday? There is a new action movie and I
think it will be fun. We can meet at 7 o’clock in front of the cinema and have pizza after the
movie.

I’m going to the park tomorrow afternoon. Would you like to come with me? We can take a
walk, sit on the grass and eat ice cream. It’s going to be a nice sunny day.

Hey, I’m planning to go to the beach next weekend. Do you want to join me? We can swim,
play volleyball and relax all day. Bring your swimsuit!

Would you like to come to my house for dinner on Friday night? I’m going to cook chicken
with rice. My sister and brother will be there too.
Let’s go shopping this afternoon. There is a big sale at the mall. We can buy some new
clothes and then have coffee at the food court.

I’m going to celebrate my birthday next Saturday. Would you like to come to my party? We
will have music, cake and many friends.

Do you want to play football with us tomorrow morning? We are meeting at 9 o’clock in the
park. It’s going to be a friendly match.

I’m thinking of going to the mountains next month. Would you like to come with me? We can
hike and take beautiful photos.

Let’s go out for dinner tonight. There is a new Mexican restaurant near my house. Would you
like to try it with me?

My family is having a barbecue this Sunday. You are invited! We are going to eat grilled meat
and listen to music in the garden.

Would you like to come to the cinema with us on Friday? We want to watch the new comedy
movie. After the movie we can go for a walk.

I’m planning to visit the zoo next weekend. Do you want to come? We can see the animals
and take lots of pictures.

Let’s meet tomorrow after class. We can go to the coffee shop and study English together for
a while.

I’m going to have a small party at my apartment next Friday night. Would you like to come?
We will listen to music and play games.

Do you want to go running with me in the morning? We can meet at 6:30 in the park and
then have breakfast together afterwards.

My friends and I are going to the amusement park on Saturday. Would you like to join us?
It’s going to be a very fun day.

Would you like to come to my house to watch the football match tonight? We can order pizza
and enjoy the game together.

Let’s go to the museum this Sunday morning. There is a new exhibition about history. I think
it will be interesting.

I’m planning a trip to the lake next month. Do you want to come with me? We can fish, swim
and camp for two days.

Would you like to go dancing with me on Saturday night? There is a good salsa club
downtown. We can have a great time.

“Would you like to go to the cinema on Saturday?” “Sure! I’d love to.”

“Let’s go to the beach next weekend.” “That sounds great! I’d love to go with you.”

“Do you want to come to my house for dinner tonight?” “I’m sorry, I can’t. I have to study.”

“We’re going to the park tomorrow. Would you like to join us?” “Sure! That sounds great!”

“Would you like to go shopping this afternoon?” “I’m sorry, I can’t today. Maybe next time.”

“Let’s have dinner together on Friday.” “I’d love to! Thank you for inviting me.”

“We’re playing football on Sunday. Do you want to come?” “Sure! I’d love to play with you.”

“Would you like to watch a movie tonight?” “I’m sorry, I can’t. I’m very tired. Maybe next
time.”

“My family is having a barbecue. You are invited!” “That sounds great! Thank you!”

“Let’s go to the mountains next month.” “Sure! I’d love to. Count me in!”
